meta.title: Your Second Chord: A Major
meta.subtitle: Learn the bright and happy A chord—just 3 fingers!

objectives: Learn the A major chord shape

steps.title: Time for chord #2!
steps.body: You already know E minor—now let's learn A MAJOR! This chord sounds bright and happy. It uses 3 fingers, all on the same fret. The cool part? You only strum 5 strings instead of all 6!

steps.title: The A major chord diagram
steps.body: Here's what A major looks like. You'll use 3 fingers—middle (2), ring (3), and pinky (4)—all on the 2nd fret. Notice the X on the thickest string? That means DON'T play that string!

steps.title: Place your first finger
steps.body: Put your MIDDLE finger (2nd finger) on the 2nd fret of the 4th string (D string). Press down firmly behind the fret. This is the same string you used for E minor!

steps.title: Add your second finger
steps.body: Put your RING finger (3rd finger) on the 2nd fret of the 3rd string (G string). Now you have two fingers side by side on the 2nd fret.

steps.title: Add your third finger
steps.body: Put your PINKY finger (4th finger) on the 2nd fret of the 2nd string (B string). All three fingers are now on the 2nd fret, lined up like little soldiers!

steps.title: Check your chord shape
steps.body: Look at your hand—you should have 3 fingers on the 2nd fret, on strings 4, 3, and 2. Your pinky should be relaxed and out of the way. The thickest string (Low E) should NOT be touched.

steps.title: Strum only 5 strings
steps.body: Here's the tricky part: Start your strum from the 5th string (A string), NOT the thickest string. Strum down across 5 strings. Try to avoid hitting that thick Low E string—it doesn't belong in this chord!

steps.title: Practice A major
steps.body: Strum your A major chord a few times. Listen for a bright, happy sound. If any strings buzz or sound muted, check that your fingers are pressing firmly and curved. Take your time—this chord takes practice!

steps.title: 🎉 You know 2 chords!
steps.body: Amazing work! You now know TWO guitar chords—E minor and A major! 🎸 That's huge! Most popular songs only use 3-4 chords, so you're already halfway to playing real music. Keep practicing these shapes until your fingers remember them!
